jobs.prompt: 16:9 abstract editorial illustration for a university mathematical analysis lecture on limits of functions (epsilon–delta viewpoint). Visual metaphor: a thin horizontal corridor suggesting a target band for values, a narrow vertical corridor suggesting proximity in the domain, and a smooth flowing ribbon passing through their crossing — function values trapped near a limit. Warm paper background #faf9f5, ink-like shapes #141413, dusty blue #6a9bcc, terracotta #d97757, olive #788c5d. Premium academic magazine style, generous negative space. Absolutely no readable text, letters, digits, formulas, axis tick labels, or UI.

16:9 abstract editorial illustration suggesting the two classic limits of analysis: oscillation nearly matching a straight guide (hinting sin x over x near 0) and gentle compounding growth toward a natural ceiling (hinting the number e). Same restrained palette (#faf9f5, #141413, #6a9bcc, #d97757, #788c5d), soft light, subtle paper grain. Abstract only: no digits, no letters, no equations, no fake charts.
